question:
Implement a function to merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted linked list. The linked lists are represented as follows: 
the head of the list is the first node, and each node contains an integer value and a reference to the next node.

Given the heads of two sorted linked lists, merge them in a way that the merged list is also sorted.

What is the time complexity and space complexity of your solution?
